title: The day of the LORD against Judah
summary_text: Zephaniah announces a sweeping day of judgment that will reach Judah, Jerusalem, and the surrounding idolatries. The chapter lists the people who will be cut off and warns that complacency will not save anyone. The prophet urges silence because the day of the LORD is near and the judgment is thorough.
key_points: The day of the LORD is declared near.
The coming judgment will be comprehensive.
evidence_phrases: the LORD has prepared a sacrifice

title: Seek the LORD before judgment falls
summary_text: Zephaniah calls the humble to seek the LORD before the decree sweeps across the nations. He pronounces judgment on Philistia, Moab, Ammon, Cush, and Assyria, showing that the LORD rules beyond Judah alone. The chapter is a warning to seek meekness while there is still time.
key_points: The humble are told to seek the LORD.
Meekness is the refuge before judgment.
evidence_phrases: He will stretch out his hand
I am, and there is none besides me

title: Jerusalem purified and restored
summary_text: The chapter begins with Jerusalem's stubborn corruption, but it ends with the LORD purifying the people and gathering a humble remnant. The nations are summoned and then scattered, while Zion is promised a song of rejoicing and a safe home under God's reign. Zephaniah closes on restoration after judgment, not because sin was small, but because God saves a cleansed people.
key_points: God purifies the speech and worship of the remnant.
The nations are gathered and then scattered.
The book ends with joy, salvation, and restoration.
evidence_phrases: woe to her who is rebellious
call on the name of the LORD
The LORD is in your midst
